decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressive plays while utilizing blink effects to repeatedly trigger valuable enters-the-battlefield abilities. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Witch Enchanter, you can efficiently control the board and generate card advantage. With powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Solitude, you can quickly overwhelm your opponent. Additionally, the deck includes a strong mana base with cards like Flagstones of Trokair and efficient removal with Prismatic Ending. The goal is to apply pressure early on and maintain board dominance with the combination of aggro threats and blink synergy.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Sire of Seven Deaths. It also utilizes control elements such as Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with large threats while controlling the board with removal spells and disruption. The combination of ramp and control elements allows the deck to quickly establish dominance and close out the game before the opponent can recover.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Allosaurus Combo by using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to tutor for key pieces. The goal is to get Allosaurus Rider onto the battlefield, then use its ability to cast and flash in Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant or Griselbrand for a powerful combo finish. The deck also includes cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to protect the combo and disrupt opponents, while also utilizing ramp and card draw to stay ahead in the game.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize cheap spells and aggressive creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By playing efficient threats like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck aims to close out the game as quickly as possible. The deck also includes card selection spells like Preordain and expressive iteration to maintain consistency and find the right cards needed for the aggressive game plan. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive nature of the deck with fetch lands and shock lands for flexibility.

This deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with blink effects to generate value and tempo advantage. By constantly returning creatures like Starfield Shepherd and Witch Enchanter to the battlefield with Ephemerate and Solitude, the deck can overwhelm opponents quickly. The inclusion of cards like Springleaf Drum and Ocelot Pride help to accelerate the deck's game plan, while cards like Prismatic Ending provide removal options. The mana base is carefully designed to ensure consistency and smooth gameplay, allowing the deck to execute its strategy efficiently.

The deck focuses on fast and aggressive Eldrazi creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It includes cards that can disrupt the opponent's strategy, such as Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void, while also providing powerful finishers like It That Heralds the End and Sire of Seven Deaths. The deck is designed to maximize the efficiency of Eldrazi creatures and take advantage of the tribal synergies provided by cards like Eldrazi Temple and Cavern of Souls. It aims to close out games before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly reanimate powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo. Additionally, the deck uses instant-speed spells like Solitude and Prismatic Ending to control the board and stay alive while setting up the combo. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and maximize consistency in executing the reanimation strategy.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells. The strategy revolves around generating value from creature cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Skyclave Apparition while maintaining board control with cards like Solitude and Static Prison. The deck also has strong synergy with cards like Ephemerate and Birthing Ritual to maximize the effectiveness of its creatures. Overall, the deck focuses on aggressive plays and tempo advantage to outpace the opponent and secure victory.

This UW Control deck focuses on maintaining control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. The deck aims to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan while using powerful planeswalkers like Teferi to generate advantage and close out the game. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds additional synergy and flexibility to the deck. Additionally, the deck includes a mix of efficient removal spells, versatile counterspells, and powerful finishers to ensure that the opponent is unable to mount a successful offense.

This deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. The deck utilizes control elements such as counterspells, removal spells, and board wipes to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Karn, the Great Creator is a key card in the deck, allowing access to powerful artifacts in the sideboard like Karn's Sylex to further control the battlefield. The deck also includes powerful Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Devourer of Destiny to close out the game quickly once control is established.

This deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between creatures with the Broodscale and Fleshraker subtype, while also featuring powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End. The deck includes a mix of efficient removal spells and artifacts to support the combo strategy. The goal is to control the board with disruptive creatures and spells, while gradually building up a threatening board presence to overwhelm the opponent. By efficiently managing resources and playing strategically, the deck aims to create a win condition through a combination of creature aggression and spell disruption.

This deck focuses on ramping up quickly with cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor to quickly play Primeval Titan and search for lands like Urza's Saga, Boseiju, Who Endures, and The Mycosynth Gardens. The goal is to assemble the Amulet Titan combo, which involves using lands like Crumbling Vestige and Gruul Turf to generate extra mana and trigger effects. Once the combo is set up, the deck can quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells like Scapeshift and Malevolent Rumble to secure victory.

This deck focuses on generating card advantage and cycling through your deck quickly to find key combo pieces. It has a strong control element with cards like Force of Negation and Subtlety to disrupt your opponent's game plan. The primary win condition is to cast Living End, which will bring all creatures from your graveyard onto the battlefield, potentially overwhelming your opponent with a large board presence. Additionally, the deck has some alternative win conditions like Colossal Skyturtle and Mistralis Village to provide different avenues to victory. The deck aims to be fast and resilient, utilizing its combo pieces efficiently to secure the win.

The deck focuses on deploying artifacts quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal and Engineered Explosives, the deck can generate a strong board presence early on. Additionally, the deck includes artifact creatures like Arcbound Ravager and Salvage Titan as win conditions, while Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides card advantage and utility. With a mixture of artifact ramp, removal, and threats, this deck aims to outpace the opponent and secure victory before they can establish their own game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly generating artifact creatures and utilizing them to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks and efficient removal spells. By exploiting the synergy between artifact creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary with powerful artifacts such as Mox Opal and Engineered Explosives, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga further enhances the deck's ability to generate value and maintain pressure on the opponent.

This deck aims to quickly ramp up mana using rituals and medallions to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Past in Flames. Wrenn's Resolve helps protect key combo pieces, while cards like Reckless Impulse provide card advantage. The deck also utilizes Wish cards to tutor for specific answers or combo pieces. The inclusion of Valakut Awakening and Glimpse the Impossible allows for card selection and digging deeper into the deck. Finally, the deck has a finisher in Grapeshot, which can be cast multiple times with Past in Flames for a lethal combo.
